source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In Fig. 2.31 two conductors of infinite length carry a current $I$. They are parallel and separated by a distance $2 a$. A circular conducting ring of radius $a$ in the plane of the parallel wires lies between the two straight conductors and is insulated from them. Find the coefficient of mutual inductance between the circular conductor and the two straight conductors.
extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object representation rather than a file path or URL

issues: metadata.image_paths contains a PIL image object representation rather than a file path or URL

image_dependency: 题目涉及两根平行长直导线与位于其间的圆环的空间几何关系，具有明显图形/空间依赖。
输入字段标注 `requires_image=true` 且 `text_dominant=false`，支持较高图像依赖判断。
资产登记显示存在 1 张主图且 `registry_passed=true`，说明上游认为图像资源可用。
但题干文字已给出关键尺寸与相对位置（距离 `2a`、圆环半径 `a`、位于两导线之间），因此不按满分处理。
multi_step: 求互感通常需要先写出长直导线磁场，再结合圆环几何求穿过圆环的总磁通，最后由定义得到互感，至少包含多步推导。
题目涉及两根导线对圆环的共同作用，需要组合两部分贡献，而非单一步代入。
题干是物理推导型开放问答，非选择题，答案为闭式表达式，通常对应中等偏高的多步性。
verifiability: 标准答案是明确的短公式 `M=2\mu_{0}a`，答案形式清晰，便于规则或符号比对。
题目给定量和几何关系较完整，理论上可依据电磁学公式复核结果。
答案不是自然语言解释而是单一解析式，因此可验证性较高。

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: In Fig. 2.31 two conductors of infinite length carry a current $I$. They are parallel and separated by a distance $2 a$. A circular conducting ring of radius $a$ in the plane of the parallel wires lies between the two straight conductors and is insulated from them. Find the coefficient of mutual inductance between the circular conductor and the two straight conductors.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object representation rather than a file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain: The magnetic field at a point between the two conductors at distance $r$ from one conductor is $$\mathbf{B}(r)=\frac{\mu_{0} I}{2 \pi}\left(\frac{1}{r}+\frac{1}{2 a-r}\right) \mathbf{e}_{\theta}.$$ So the magnetic flux crossing the area of the ring is given by $$\begin{aligned} \phi & =\int \mathbf{B} \cdot d \mathbf{S}=2 \int_{0}^{a} B(r) \cdot 2 \sqrt{a^{2}-(a-r)^{2}}\, dr \\ & =2 \int_{0}^{a} \frac{\mu_{0} I}{2 \pi}\left(\frac{1}{r}+\frac{1}{2 a-r}\right) \cdot 2 \sqrt{a^{2}-(a-r)^{2}}\, dr. \end{aligned}$$ Let $x=a-r$ and integrate: $$\begin{aligned} \phi & =2 \int_{0}^{a} \frac{\mu_{0} I}{\pi}\left(\frac{1}{a-x}+\frac{1}{a+x}\right) \sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}}\, dx \\ & =\left.\frac{4 \mu_{0} I a}{\pi} \arcsin\frac{x}{a}\right|_{0}^{a}=2 \mu_{0} I a. \end{aligned}$$ Hence the coefficient of mutual inductance is $$M=\frac{\phi}{I}=2 \mu_{0} a.$$

problem_main_record.reasoning_chain: The magnetic field at a point between the two conductors at distance $r$ from one conductor is $$\mathbf{B}(r)=\frac{\mu_{0} I}{2 \pi}\left(\frac{1}{r}+\frac{1}{2 a-r}\right) \mathbf{e}_{\theta}.$$ So the magnetic flux crossing the area of the ring is given by $$\begin{aligned} \phi & =\int \mathbf{B} \cdot d \mathbf{S}=2 \int_{0}^{a} B(r) \cdot 2 \sqrt{a^{2}-(a-r)^{2}}\, dr \\ & =2 \int_{0}^{a} \frac{\mu_{0} I}{2 \pi}\left(\frac{1}{r}+\frac{1}{2 a-r}\right) \cdot 2 \sqrt{a^{2}-(a-r)^{2}}\, dr. \end{aligned}$$ Let $x=a-r$ and integrate: $$\begin{aligned} \phi & =2 \int_{0}^{a} \frac{\mu_{0} I}{\pi}\left(\frac{1}{a-x}+\frac{1}{a+x}\right) \sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}}\, dx \\ & =\left.\frac{4 \mu_{0} I a}{\pi} \arcsin\frac{x}{a}\right|_{0}^{a}=2 \mu_{0} I a. \end{aligned}$$ Hence the coefficient of mutual inductance is $$M=\frac{\phi}{I}=2 \mu_{0} a.$$
